hypotheses.label: MOpt's corpus reaches the gate keyword but drives the Lemon parser shallower per seed than naive's, never iterating deep enough to fire the reduce

params.note: runs the 7 resolving (naive-side, winner) vs 10 blocking (mopt-side, loser) seeds through the cov image; reads per-seed-normalized execution count + blocked-side count at yy_reduce:157670

hypotheses.prediction: naive's winner seeds drive yy_reduce:157670 MEASURABLY DEEPER per seed than mopt's blocker seeds (winner_deeper=true, depth_lift high), AND the blocked true-side count is reached by the winner corpus but not the loser corpus (side_lift high). The card observes naive seeds fire the true side (T=9) and exercise the reduce path hundreds of times while mopt seeds bail early at T=0. This rules out the alternative that BOTH corpora drive the parser equally deep and the divergence is a pure terminal-resolve artifact (under which depth_lift ~= 1, winner_deeper=false). It is a genuinely different mechanism from the refuted keyword-assembly story: the prior token_count test did NOT find 'SELECT' depleted in mopt's corpus, so the deficit is not keyword PRESENCE but parse DEPTH / reduce-iteration count behind the keyword.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: rules out the null that mopt and naive corpora reach the reduce action at equal per-seed depth (depth_lift ~= 1, winner_deeper=false) — i.e. that the resolve/block split is not reflected in how deep each corpus actually drives the parser. A falsified prediction (mopt seeds reach yy_reduce as deep as naive's) would say the depth-deficit story is wrong and the mechanism is non-discriminable here.
hypotheses.g2_independence: depth_reach reads the per-seed-normalized llvm-cov execution count of the branch line from a NEUTRAL cov binary — it measures how deep each fuzzer's CORPUS drives the parser, not whether the campaign labeled the branch resolved/blocked. The resolve label was assigned by the ≥8/8 W/L campaign rule; this signal is the independent corpus-trajectory measurement (G2).

hypotheses.label: MOpt operator scheduling under-assembles the gate keyword that naive's havoc reaches

params.scope: saved corpus seeds for the branch (sqlite3 has on-disk corpus)
params.token_lift_def: median naive_tokens / max(median mopt_tokens, epsilon) at the gate keyword

hypotheses.prediction: mopt's saved corpus contains FEWER occurrences of the gate keyword 'SELECT' (plus its long-expression body) than naive's, i.e. mopt_tokens < naive_tokens. If MOpt's scheduling truly underweights the token-insert/replace operators needed to assemble the keyword, the keyword should be depleted in mopt's corpus relative to naive's; an alternative in which both reach the keyword equally (scheduling irrelevant) predicts mopt_tokens ~= naive_tokens.
hypotheses.g1_alternative_ruled_out: the null that MOpt scheduling does not affect keyword assembly (mopt and naive corpora carry the keyword at parity); under that null token_lift ~= 1.
hypotheses.g2_independence: token_count over saved corpus BYTE content is independent of the resolve/block label that defined the shape — it measures what each fuzzer's corpus contains, not whether the blocker branch fired. It does not 'verify mopt-blocks with mopt-blocks'.

data_realities_respected.no_mutation_op_lineage: MOpt scheduling (a probability distribution over operators) is INVISIBLE in mutation_op — the havoc/token vocabulary is byte-identical across all 4 fuzzers (data_reality #1). NEITHER hypothesis proposes a seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence 'MOpt scheduling event'. Both measure only the downstream CONSEQUENCE in what the corpus reaches (corpus content token_count, or per-seed parse depth), never the scheduling cause directly.
data_realities_respected.corpus_available: sqlite3 is one of the 4 on-disk-corpus targets (data_reality #4), and the branch has 7 resolving + 10 blocking seeds in db/blockers.sqlite (data_reality #5), so both the seed_set token_count and seed_set depth_reach descriptors can score this branch.
data_realities_respected.alignment_safe: this is a state_or_grammar gate with variable-size winner/loser seeds (data_reality #3), so byte-offset alignment is NOT assumed: operand_enrichment/byte_freq_ratio is deliberately NOT used (it would return no_gate_signature on a structural gate). token_count is substring occurrence; depth_reach is per-seed execution count — neither assumes a fixed byte offset.
data_realities_respected.cov_image_present: depth_reach needs the libafl-sqlite3-cov image (present) and runs Docker per-branch (~30-60s); scope_limits satisfied.

notes: Target sqlite3, single branch (sqlite3_14748), gate yy_reduce:157670 — input must begin with 'SELECT' (53 45 4c 45 43 54) plus a long well-formed expression body to reach the deep Lemon-parser reduce. PRIOR: the round-0 hypothesis (mopt_scheduling_keyword_assembly_deficit, token_count) was scored rule_not_met — 'SELECT' was NOT found depleted in mopt's corpus relative to naive's (token_lift>=1.5 AND mopt<naive did not hold). Per the superset/monotonic rule it is KEPT verbatim as the second-priority hypothesis (it carries no confirmed branch but the brief marks it KEEP). NEW MECHANISM (now primary): mopt_shallow_parse_depth_deficit. The token_count refutation is INFORMATIVE — it shows the deficit is NOT keyword PRESENCE (mopt's corpus does carry 'SELECT'), so the genuinely-different alternative is parse DEPTH: mopt's seeds reach the keyword but drive the Lemon reduce loop shallower per seed (the card: naive fires the true side T=9 and exercises the reduce path hundreds of times; mopt seeds bail early, T=0). depth_reach measures exactly this per-seed execution-count depth at yy_reduce:157670 from a neutral cov binary — built tool, sqlite3 cov image present, seeds in DB, G1+G2 hold. decision_order puts depth first (it is the surviving discriminating signal after token_count refuted). HONEST CAVEAT (G3): the ROOT cause — MOpt's particle-swarm OPERATOR-SCHEDULING distribution — remains non-observable in the data (data_reality #1: scheduling leaves no mutation_op trace, identical vocabulary across all 4 fuzzers). BOTH tests are CONSEQUENCE proxies: they can confirm 'mopt's corpus drove the parser shallower than naive's' but cannot attribute that to scheduling specifically vs any other single-trial corpus-trajectory difference at n=1. If depth_reach finds NO depth gap (winner_deeper=false or depth_lift<2.0) AND token_count again fails, the branch correctly falls to fallback:inconclusive — for a non-local adaptive-mutation-scheduling family that terminal-corpus tools cannot fully attribute, an honest inconclusive is the correct outcome (G3); we do NOT force a label. No sub-group is split (single branch, single mechanism story with two consequence-level probes ordered by discriminating power).
